reasons.code_quality: Clean, idiomatic React + TypeScript. Proper interfaces for FormFields and FormErrors, extracted validateEmail function, correct event types (React.ChangeEvent, React.FormEvent), 'use client' directive in place, noValidate on form, and role='alert' on error paragraphs shows accessibility awareness. Inline styles are the only mild detractor, but the task explicitly asks for minimal/no-UI-library, so this is appropriate.
reasons.completeness: Every requirement and acceptance criterion is satisfied: file at app/profile/edit/page.tsx, default export, name input with min-length-2 validation, email input with regex validation, inline error messages below each field, success message reading exactly 'Profile updated', React+TypeScript only, no new packages, no modifications to lib/supabase.ts, next.config.js, or package.json. No unauthorized additions.
reasons.edge_cases: Name field uses .trim() before length check, catching whitespace-only inputs. Both fields validated simultaneously and all errors shown together. Success flag resets on any field change. Email regex /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/ catches empty string and malformed addresses. Minor gaps: no maxLength attribute on inputs and no per-field live validation on blur (errors only surface on submit), but neither is required by the spec.
reasons.security: No backend calls, no SQL, no secrets in the new file. React JSX rendering naturally escapes user-supplied strings, eliminating XSS risk in the error/success display. The stub anon key in lib/supabase.ts is pre-existing seed code and not a real credential. No meaningful attack surface remains for a pure client-side form without persistence.

overall_notes: Exemplary minimal submission — hits every acceptance criterion cleanly, respects scope boundaries, and adds small quality touches (trim, role=alert, noValidate) beyond the bare minimum. Nothing to penalise.
